title: There Will Be Blood
logline: A ruthless silver prospector transforms into an oil tycoon in turn-of-the-century California, where his ambition and misanthropy lead him into conflict with a young preacher, ultimately destroying everyone around him including his adopted son.
antagonistOrConflict: Eli Sunday (preacher) and Daniel's own misanthropy and greed
themes: False prophets and religion as performance

actBreaks.description: Daniel arrives in Little Boston and begins his campaign to acquire the Sunday ranch land, shifting from lone prospector to calculating businessman.

validation.ruling: Daniel and H.W. departing the Sunday ranch after securing the initial deal represents an irreversible plot shift at 25%—Daniel has committed to the Little Boston venture, Eli has shown his antagonistic nature by pushing H.W., and the story fundamentally transitions from negotiation to exploitation, with no possibility of returning to the pre-deal state.

actBreaks.description: Daniel sends H.W. away on the train after the fire, severing his primary human connection and fully embracing isolation.

validation.ruling: Daniel abandoning H.W. on the train at 62% is the ultimate irreversible character decision and thematic shift—Daniel chooses oil empire over fatherhood, fundamentally transforming from protective father to isolated tyrant, forcing the story into its final act of complete moral dissolution with no possibility of redemption.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a character descent structured around Daniel's progressive dehumanization. Act One (scenes 1-32) establishes Daniel as a brutal but functional capitalist—he's violent, calculating, but still tethered to H.W. and capable of partnership. The arrival at Little Boston marks the shift into Act Two (scenes 33-84), where Daniel's empire-building collides with Eli Sunday's religious ambition, creating the central rivalry. The oil gusher that deafens H.W. becomes the catalyst for Daniel's emotional collapse. Act Two ends when Daniel literally abandons his son on the train—the moment he chooses oil over humanity. Act Three (scenes 85-123) tracks Daniel's complete moral disintegration: he murders the imposter Henry, is humiliated into baptism by Eli, achieves total business victory, and finally commits murder in his bowling alley tomb. The structure mirrors Daniel's arc: methodical accumulation, explosive crisis, hollow triumph.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script's pacing is deliberately patient in Act One, using nearly wordless sequences (scenes 2-5, the silver mining prologue) to establish Daniel's solitary brutality. Act Two accelerates once drilling begins (scene 43 onward), building tension through the rivalry with Eli and culminating in the catastrophic gusher (scene 57). The Henry Brands subplot (scenes 78-102) creates a false hope of connection before the murder sequence, which is given extraordinary space to breathe. The final act moves in fits—long stretches of time pass between scenes 111-120, then the script slows to excruciating real-time for the final confrontation (scene 123), which occupies nearly 9 pages of a single location.

incitingIncident.description: A young man named Paul Sunday arrives at Daniel's office in the Bankside house and tells Daniel he knows a place with oil where land can be bought cheaply; Daniel questions him about the location and what he knows, and Paul reveals it's his family's ranch, asking what the information is worth and requesting five hundred dollars plus ten percent of profits.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. PLAINVIEW AMERICA DRILLING CO. (BANKSIDE HOUSE)

validation.ruling: Paul Sunday's arrival offering information about oil-rich land that can be bought cheaply shatters Daniel's current drilling operation status quo and establishes the main conflict/goal of acquiring the Sunday ranch, occurring at the ideal 10% position and forcing Daniel into irreversible action toward Little Boston.

midpoint.description: Men battle the blazing gusher as Daniel barks directions; they snap guide wires to topple the burning derrick, then approach with metal sheets to bang dirt into the cellar wall.

validation.ruling: The gusher fire represents a false defeat that becomes Daniel's point of no return—he shifts from reactive drilling to proactive dominance, declaring 'we've got an ocean of oil underneath your feet and not a soul can go near it but me,' demonstrating his transformation from cautious businessman to ruthless monopolist at precisely 45% into the script.

climax.description: Eli finds Daniel passed out drunk in his bowling alley and wakes him, then reveals he needs money because his congregation has abandoned him and asks Daniel to help him drill on the Bandy tract, but Daniel mocks him, forces Eli to renounce God and admit he's a false prophet, then beats Eli to death with a bowling pin.

validation.ruling: The bowling alley confrontation between Daniel and Eli at 93% represents the ultimate confrontation where Daniel directly faces his antagonist, resolves the central power struggle through violence, demonstrates his complete moral degradation, and pays off the religious/capitalist conflict that has driven the entire narrative.

scenes.heading: 2 EXT. NEW MEXICO DESERT - DAY - 1898 2
scenes.summary: Daniel Plainview digs alone in a fifteen-foot shaft in the New Mexico desert, searching for silver in extreme heat.

scenes.heading: 3 EXT. DESERT - EVENING. 3
scenes.summary: Daniel walks beside his mule pulling a cart of prospector's supplies across rough desert terrain.

scenes.heading: 4 EXT. MINE SHAFT- ANOTHER DAY. 4
scenes.summary: Daniel continues digging in a now twenty-foot-deep shaft, climbs out when he hears something, discovers his mule has dropped dead from the heat, and returns to work.

scenes.heading: 5 INT. SHAFT. LATER.
scenes.summary: Daniel discovers a glistening vein of silver ore in the rock, loads massive chunks into his cart after unhitching the dead mule, then falls while descending back into the shaft.
scenes.turningPointNote: Daniel discovers silver and suffers a serious fall that breaks his leg, forcing him to push forward despite injury

scenes.heading: 8 EXT. DESERT. DAY 8
scenes.summary: Daniel pushes the cart with his upper body then drags himself to catch up, repeating this across the desert floor toward a distant town a mile away.

scenes.heading: 9 INT. SILVER ASSAY OFFICE/LEASE OFFICE - DAY. 9
scenes.summary: Daniel lies on his back in a silver assay office while his ore is processed and valued, preparing to sell the lease.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOMEWHERE IN CALIFORNIA. DAY -A FEW YEARS LATER- 1902
scenes.summary: A few years later in California, Daniel works with a crew including Ailman and his one-year-old son H.W., digging a well using picks, axes, and a pulley system.

scenes.heading: 13 EXT. DRILLING SITE - DAY 13
scenes.summary: The crew erects a crude twenty-foot derrick with a drill fashioned from a eucalyptus tree; Daniel operates it successfully on the first hit, but on the second attempt the 300-pound apparatus breaks loose and plummets down the hole.

scenes.heading: 14 INT. HOLE. - THAT MOMENT 14
scenes.summary: The drill falls straight down the hole and splinters apart; Daniel looks down after it then back at the rig.

scenes.heading: 15 INT. HOLE. 15
scenes.summary: Ailman descends into the dark, muddy hole wearing a rubber slicker and wrestles the drill free by kicking it repeatedly until it breaks loose and can be pulled up.

scenes.heading: 16 EXT. HOLE. DAY. 16
scenes.summary: The crew drills again and hits something very hard; they pull up the drill log to find it absolutely soaked in oil measuring ten feet up the wood, and Daniel's breathing becomes heavier and crazier as he examines it.
scenes.turningPointNote: Daniel strikes oil for the first time, fundamentally changing his prospects and setting his path as an oilman

scenes.heading: 17 EXT. DRILLING SITE. DAYS LATER. 17
scenes.summary: The crew dumps oil into a reservoir then transfers it into various containers; Ailman works while holding the one-year-old H.W. in his arms.

scenes.heading: 18 INT. SHAFT.
scenes.summary: Daniel and Ailman work in the shaft moving in and out of consciousness; Daniel hears something and looks up to see timber supports coming loose, then a large sharp piece cracks and flies straight down like a dagger into the top of Ailman's head, killing him instantly.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ailman is killed in a drilling accident, leaving his infant son H.W. orphaned and in Daniel's care

scenes.heading: 19 EXT. DRILLING SITE. DAY 19
scenes.summary: Daniel looks at baby H.W. in the basket, picks him up and holds him awkwardly, then puts him back and continues looking at the child.

scenes.heading: 20 INT. FLOP HOUSE. NIGHT 20
scenes.summary: Baby H.W. cries loudly in a flophouse while Daniel sleeps; Daniel wakes, puts whiskey on his finger and in the baby's mouth, then carries the crying child to a mother who nurses him while Daniel falls back asleep.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN. MOVING. DAY.
scenes.summary: Six years later in 1908, Daniel delivers a speech to potential investors about his oil wells and income, presenting himself as a family man with his son and offering them a partnership opportunity with detailed financial terms.

scenes.heading: 23 EXT. SIGNAL HILL - THAT MOMENT. 23
scenes.summary: Daniel and H.W. leave a bungalow while Prescott pleads with them to stay; Daniel refuses the lease citing too much confusion and walks away despite Prescott's protests.

scenes.heading: 24 INT. BANKSIDE HOME - NIGHT. 24
scenes.summary: Daniel meets with the Bankside family and offers to buy their ranch for thirty-seven hundred dollars with a ninety-nine year lease allowing them to stay and farm, explaining his drilling plans and timeline.

scenes.heading: 25 EXT. DERRICK FLOOR - EVENING - WEEKS LATER. 25
scenes.summary: Daniel guides the drilling process on a derrick erected on top of the Bankside home with his team of drillers; after a deep rumble and quiet moment, a burst of gas, water, and oil explodes straight up.

scenes.heading: INT. PLAINVIEW AMERICA DRILLING CO. (BANKSIDE HOUSE)
scenes.summary: A young man named Paul Sunday arrives at Daniel's office in the Bankside house and tells Daniel he knows a place with oil where land can be bought cheaply; Daniel questions him about the location and what he knows, and Paul reveals it's his family's ranch, asking what the information is worth and requesting five hundred dollars plus ten percent of profits.
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul Sunday provides the tip that will lead Daniel to Little Boston and the Sunday ranch, setting the central conflict in motion

scenes.heading: 27 EXT. TRAIN DEPOT/LITTLE BOSTON, CALIFORNIA- LATE DAY. 27
scenes.summary: Daniel and H.W. arrive by car at the Little Boston train depot, unload camping equipment, and begin walking toward the nearby town and hills.

scenes.heading: 29 EXT. SHEEP ROAD/BASE OF HILLS- DUSK. 29
scenes.summary: Daniel and H.W. walk with heavy camping equipment; Daniel reminds H.W. to remember his silence and walk beside him, then they encounter Abel Sunday on the road and exchange greetings.

scenes.heading: 30 EXT. SUNDAY RANCH/MOMENTS LATER. 30
scenes.summary: Daniel and H.W. arrive at the Sunday ranch and meet the family including Mother Sunday, daughters Ruth and Mary, and son Eli; Daniel asks to set up a tent, Abel offers hospitality, and the family begins helping despite Daniel's insistence they can manage themselves.

scenes.heading: 31 INT. SUNDAY RANCH/NIGHT - LATER. 31
scenes.summary: Daniel and H.W. sit by the fire with Abel Sunday and his family; Abel asks if Daniel is saved and questions him about his church, leading to a tense exchange where Daniel deflects by citing scripture about not discussing faith with strangers, and Abel responds that they're no longer strangers.

scenes.heading: 32 EXT. SUNDAY RANCH - DAWN 32
scenes.summary: Daniel and H.W. put out their morning fire, collect their hunting gear, and head off.

scenes.heading: 33 EXT. HILLS NEARBY/CANYON - MORNING. 33
scenes.summary: Daniel and H.W. hunt quail in the hills; H.W. shoots successfully, runs to retrieve the birds, stumbles on something, recognizes it, and runs back to tell his father he found oil.
scenes.turningPointNote: H.W. discovers oil on the Sunday property, confirming Paul's tip and validating Daniel's journey to Little Boston

scenes.heading: 34 EXT. FIELD - MOMENTS LATER. 34
scenes.summary: Daniel and H.W. examine a patch of tar and oil slick bubbling on the surface; Daniel tests it with a stick and matches, successfully igniting it, and identifies it as earthquake-released material.

scenes.heading: 35 EXT. NEARBY HILL - MOMENTS LATER 35
scenes.summary: Daniel and H.W. climb a hill and survey the landscape of rolling, rocky hills; Daniel walks feeling the ground beneath his feet.

scenes.heading: 36 EXT. BANDY RANCH/NEARBY CANYON - LATER 36
scenes.summary: Daniel and H.W. discover a rundown home with pigs in a pen surrounded by foul-smelling sulphur water; Daniel explains the sulphur kills fleas and cures mange, identifying this as a good drilling location, then they enter the empty house and find religious artifacts on the walls.

scenes.heading: 37 EXT. HILLS. LATER/LATE AFTERNOON. 37
scenes.summary: Daniel and H.W. walk quickly carrying quail while Daniel excitedly explains his plan to build a pipeline to the sea, make a deal with Union Oil, eliminate railroad and shipping costs, and make real money.

scenes.heading: 38 INT. SUNDAY RANCH - DUSK.
scenes.summary: During dinner at the Sunday ranch, Daniel asks Abel about a fair price for the lot; Abel excitedly calls Daniel a prophet sent by the Lord and suggests five dollars an acre, Eli counters with six, and Daniel offers to buy the ranch for thirty-seven hundred dollars with a ninety-nine year lease allowing the family to stay and farm, which Abel accepts despite Mother Sunday's objections.
scenes.turningPointNote: Daniel successfully negotiates the purchase of the Sunday ranch, securing the central oil property and establishing his relationship with the Sunday family

scenes.heading: 39 EXT. TRAIN DEPOT/LITTLE BOSTON - MORNING (START MUSIC)
scenes.summary: Eli drives Daniel and H.W. by cart to the train depot; when H.W. climbs down, Eli gives him a push that sends him to the ground, Daniel checks if he's alright, and Eli comments on Daniel's nice car before being dismissed.

scenes.heading: 40 INT. ROSE REAL ESTATE, INSURANCE, LOANS OFFICE - MORNING. 40
scenes.summary: Daniel and H.W. meet with real estate agent Al Rose to examine a map of the area; Daniel inquires about neighboring properties including the Bandy tract and other lots, asking about prices and availability, and Rose confirms most can be acquired at six dollars an acre.

scenes.heading: 41 INT. TRAIN DEPOT - DAY - LATER.
scenes.summary: Daniel arranges for a water well man to meet H.W. at the Sunday ranch, then places a telephone call to Fletcher while H.W. watches a train approach the station; Daniel speaks with Fletcher about purchasing surrounding lots and instructs him to use different names and keep Daniel's identity secret.

scenes.heading: 42 EXT. SUNDAY RANCH. DAYS LATER. LATE AFTERNOON.
scenes.summary: H.W. supervises the water well man while talking with Mary Sunday; Fletcher and geologist Fred English inspect the landscape and take measurements; Mary asks H.W. about potential earnings and he repeatedly says it depends.

scenes.heading: 43 INT. TENT/SUNDAY RANCH. NIGHT. 43
scenes.summary: In their tent at night, H.W. tells Daniel that Mary said her father beats her if she doesn't do the praying; Daniel asks about Mary's age, praises H.W.'s work on the water well, and they exchange expressions of love before H.W. asks about the other lots they acquired.

scenes.heading: 44 INT. ROSE REAL ESTATE. DAY 44
scenes.summary: Al Rose congratulates Daniel on successfully purchasing 10,400 acres at five and a half dollars per acre, but reveals that Mr. Bandy, who owns the tract next to the Sunday Ranch, wasn't interested in selling and wants to speak with Daniel personally without knowing his name.

scenes.heading: 45 EXT. TRAIN DEPOT. DAY 45
scenes.summary: Daniel's workers including Murgins, Ghunda, and Ben Blaut arrive by train at the depot.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUNDAY RANCH. NEW ROAD. DAY. 46
scenes.summary: Teams of horses carry a huge iron steam engine and tons of long metal pipe along a newly made road, leading to a freshly built eighty-foot derrick with a small basecamp of tents and housing for twenty workers located two hundred yards from the Sunday house.

scenes.heading: INT. ABANDONED RANCH HOUSE/NEW PLAINVIEW HOUSE. DAY.
scenes.summary: H.W. oversees the cleaning and furnishing of an abandoned ranch house with help from the Sunday women, making suggestions and clearing weeds.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUNDAY RANCH/DERRICK AREA. DAY.
scenes.summary: Eli moves around the construction site with church followers handing out cloth buttons and inviting workers to the Church of the Third Revelation; Daniel watches him carefully, then Eli visits Daniel's office asking for a word and requesting five thousand dollars to bless the well and another ten thousand for the church, which Daniel refuses, offering only to pay standard property taxes.

scenes.heading: 50 INT. ABANDONED RANCH HOUSE/NEW PLAINIEW COTTAGE. DAY 50
scenes.summary: Daniel praises H.W. for doing a good job on the cleaned-up house, asks if he's ready, and they exit together.

scenes.heading: 51 EXT. THE ERECTED DERRICK - LATE AFTERNOON. 51
scenes.summary: At the spudding-in ceremony with townspeople and landowners gathered, Daniel begins a speech welcoming everyone; he notices Eli arriving with church followers, and Eli interrupts to ask if he can bless the well, which Daniel allows.

scenes.heading: EXT. DERRICK AREA. SUNDAY FARM. DUSK. 52
scenes.summary: After a small celebration dinner, Daniel speaks to young Mary in front of Abel, complimenting her new dress and asking if she's happy he came, then warns her father not to hit her anymore and promises to take care of her, leaning in and kissing her on the cheek.

scenes.heading: 53 INT. PLANVIEW COTTAGE - NIGHT 53
scenes.summary: Ben Blaut enters the cottage at night and struggles to wake the sleeping Daniel; when Daniel finally opens his eyes, Ben tells him a man fell into the well ten minutes ago, identifies him as Joe Ghunda, and Daniel asks when they're bringing him up.

scenes.heading: 55 INT. PLAINVIEW COTTAGE - CONTD. 55
scenes.summary: Daniel asks Ben if he can hear anything; Ben says no, and Daniel concludes the man has drowned in the mud.

scenes.heading: 56 INT. THE WELL. 56
scenes.summary: The camera shows the image of a man dying beneath the mud inside the well.

scenes.heading: INT. PLAINVIEW COTTAGE - CONTD. 57
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. DERRICK - NIGHT. 58
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: 59 INT. LIVING QUARTERS. MORNING. 59
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: 60 INT. ELI'S CHURCH. DAY 60
scenes.summary: Eli preaches aggressively in his church about atonement through blood, mocking an infidel who questioned the doctrine.

scenes.heading: 61 INT. CHURCH. LATER. 61
scenes.summary: Daniel asks Eli to speak at Joe Ghunda's burial, but Eli blames the accident on the unblessed well and Daniel's overworked men, creating friction between them.

scenes.heading: 62 EXT. DERRICK FLOOR - THAT MOMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: The drill breaks 900 feet down, forcing the crew to begin the laborious process of lifting and unscrewing all 900 feet of pipe in sections.

scenes.heading: 63 EXT. DERRICK - NIGHT.
scenes.summary: Hours later at night, the broken piece is retrieved and Daniel kicks it off the derrick, ordering the crew to fish for the other half.

scenes.heading: 64 EXT. DERRICK - NIGHT.
scenes.summary: The night crew fishes for the broken piece while Daniel and Abel talk; Abel questions whether Daniel would be happier if he were a backslider from faith.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUNDAY DRILLING AREA. DAY. 65
scenes.summary: Daniel walks around the Sunday drilling area, smiling at HW and another child playing nearby.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUNDAY DRILLING AREA. AT THE DERRICK. DAY. 66
scenes.summary: Workers use a jar tool to shake loose stuck casing when gas suddenly rockets out of the earth with tremendous force and a high squeal, catching HW on the derrick.
scenes.turningPointNote: The gas blowout that will deafen HW — a catastrophic accident that changes Daniel's relationship with his son and accelerates his descent.

scenes.heading: 67 INT. MESS HALL. THAT MOMENT 67
scenes.summary: Daniel brings HW into the mess hall where the boy screams hysterically that he can't hear his voice; Daniel tries to calm him but HW is panicking.

scenes.heading: 68 EXT. BASECAMP - THAT MOMENT 68
scenes.summary: Daniel exits to see the oil on fire and spewing into the air, and he begins to glow with excitement while HW remains in shock inside.

scenes.heading: 69 EXT. GUSHER. THAT MOMENT.
scenes.summary: Men battle the blazing gusher as Daniel barks directions; they snap guide wires to topple the burning derrick, then approach with metal sheets to bang dirt into the cellar wall.

scenes.heading: 70 INT. MESS HALL. LATER. 70
scenes.summary: Daniel returns to the mess hall covered in oil, mud, and ash, and leans down to look at HW being tended by the Sundays.

scenes.heading: 71 INT. PLAINVIEW COTTAGE. NIGHT, LATER. 71
scenes.summary: Daniel rocks the hysterical, crying HW in his arms through the night, whispering the alphabet and numbers and telling him he loves him.

scenes.heading: INT. PLAINVIEW COTTAGE. MORNING.
scenes.summary: A local doctor tries to examine HW but the boy violently kicks and punches him away while Daniel attempts to hold him down.

scenes.heading: INT. PLAINVIEW COTTAGE. LATER/MORNING. 73
scenes.summary: With HW asleep, Daniel meets with Fletcher and Al Rose about reservoir and drilling plans, then asks Fletcher to contact Bob Brodie to find a teacher for HW.

scenes.heading: 74 INT. DANIEL'S OFFICE/BASECAMP. DAYS LATER. 74
scenes.summary: Daniel meets with teacher Fannie Clark who suggests sending HW to her San Francisco school, but Daniel insists on building a school locally and hiring her exclusively.

scenes.heading: 75 INT. A GIANT RESERVOIR UNDER CONSTRUCTION- DAY. 75
scenes.summary: Daniel slaps Eli brutally across the face three times in the reservoir construction site, mocking his inability to heal HW and blaming him for not blessing the well.
scenes.turningPointNote: Daniel's violent humiliation of Eli — a brutal power assertion that deepens their enmity and foreshadows their final confrontation.

scenes.heading: INT. SUNDAY RANCH. NIGHT. 76
scenes.summary: At dinner, Eli's face bruised from the beating, he calls his father Abel a stupid man for letting Daniel walk all over them and missing their chance at wealth.

scenes.heading: 77 EXT. TRAIN DEPOT/NEAR LITTLE BOSTON. ANOTHER DAY. 77
scenes.summary: A male stranger steps off a train near Little Boston and walks toward the town, passing newly erected derricks on the surrounding hills.

scenes.heading: 78 EXT. PLAINVIEW COTTAGE. EVENING. 78
scenes.summary: The stranger waits at Daniel's cottage and introduces himself as Henry Plainview from Fond du Lac, claiming to be Daniel's half-brother through their father Ernest and Marion Brands.
scenes.turningPointNote: Henry's arrival — a supposed family connection that will temporarily soften Daniel before the devastating revelation of the imposture.

scenes.heading: 79 INT. PLAINVIEW COTTAGE. DAY. LATER. 79
scenes.summary: Henry eats ravenously while Daniel questions him about his background; HW watches them in silence from Daniel's lap, the sound dropping in and out from his perspective.

scenes.heading: 80 INT. PLAINVIEW COTTAGE - LATER - NIGHT. 80
scenes.summary: Daniel carries the passed-out HW to bed, then sits with Henry and directly asks what he wants; Henry says he'd like to stay and work.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIELDS - NIGHT - LATER.
scenes.summary: Daniel and Henry drink from a flask in the fields at night, discussing their father and mother; Daniel confesses his competition, hatred of people, and envy while Henry claims that part of him is gone.

scenes.heading: 82 INT. PLAINVIEW COTTAGE. MORNING. 82
scenes.summary: HW searches through Henry's bag and examines a small leather journal, holding it upside down as he tries to make sense of it.

scenes.heading: 83 EXT. BURNED DRILLING AREA. DAY.
scenes.summary: HW walks alone around the burned-out remains of the derrick.

scenes.heading: 84 INT. ELI'S CHURCH. DAY.
scenes.summary: HW sits in the empty church with Mary Sunday, who tries to talk with him and entertain him.

scenes.heading: INT. BAR. LITTLE BOSTON. NIGHT. 85
scenes.summary: Daniel and Henry drink together in a crowded bar while Fletcher watches them from across the room.

scenes.heading: 86 INT. SUNDAY RANCH HOUSE. NIGHT. 86
scenes.summary: Mother Sunday gives HW goat's milk mixed with whisky while Eli enters and looks at the boy.

scenes.heading: 87 INT. BAR - CONTINUED. 87
scenes.summary: A local woman approaches Daniel and sits near him in the bar.

scenes.heading: 88 INT. SUNDAY HOUSE. THAT MOMENT • 88
scenes.summary: Eli sits close to HW and tells him directly that his father has provoked God and he should save himself before it's too late, despite Mary saying HW can't hear.

scenes.heading: 89 EXT. ALLEY WAY/OUTSIDE BAR. NIGHT
scenes.summary: Daniel has sex with the woman in an alley behind the bar, then pays her and leaves.

scenes.heading: 90 INT. PLAINVIEW COTTAGE. NIGHT - LATER.
scenes.summary: HW pours crude oil around Henry's bed and trails it into the main house, then strikes it with a match, causing an instant fire that engulfs Henry's room in flames.
scenes.turningPointNote: HW's arson attempt — a desperate, violent act against the interloper that forces Daniel to send him away.

scenes.heading: 91 EXT. PLAINVIEW COTTAGE. NIGHT. THAT MOMENT. 91
scenes.summary: Daniel holds HW by the arms outside the burning cottage and shouts in his face, demanding to know what he's doing to them.

scenes.heading: 92 EXT. TRAIN DEPOT. LITTLE BOSTON. DAY. 92
scenes.summary: Daniel and HW, both dressed for travel, board a train together at the depot.

scenes.heading: 93 INT. TRAIN. MOMENTS LATER. 93
scenes.summary: Daniel hugs and kisses HW repeatedly on the train, then tells him he'll be right back and leaves; HW watches through the window as Daniel walks away and the train begins moving.
scenes.turningPointNote: Daniel abandons HW to the school — a betrayal that severs their bond and marks Daniel's choice of ambition over fatherhood.

scenes.heading: 95 INT. MESS HALL. NIGHT. 95
scenes.summary: Drunk and barely conscious, Daniel tells Henry that HW is not his son and that his body doesn't work to make children, then asks if Henry's does.

scenes.heading: 96 INT. TRAIN - MOVING - LATER. 96
scenes.summary: Fletcher holds and calms HW on the moving train, petting his head and speaking softly to him.

scenes.heading: 97 INT. LITTLE BOSTON TRAIN DEPOT. DAY 97
scenes.summary: Daniel and Henry meet with Tilford and Carter from Standard Oil at the train depot; Standard offers to buy Daniel's tracts and make him a millionaire, but Daniel refuses to sell.

scenes.heading: 98 INT. DANIEL'S TENT. NIGHT - DAYS LATER
scenes.summary: Daniel packs in a trance, telling Fletcher they won't have to grovel to Standard Oil; when Fletcher asks how much Daniel knows about Henry, Daniel says he's his brother.

scenes.heading: 99 EXT. BASECAMP. DAWN. (start music/building)
scenes.summary: Daniel and Henry saddle three horses with camping supplies, hunting weapons, and wrapped crude oil samples, then ride off into the hills.

scenes.heading: 100 EXT. BANDY'S LAND. MORNING.
scenes.summary: Daniel and Henry drive wood stakes with red markers into the ground to mark a pipeline route, then encounter William Bandy who says his grandfather isn't interested in drilling.

scenes.heading: 102 EXT. LARGE FLAT FIELD. DAY.
scenes.summary: Daniel and Henry plant a wood stake with a red flag in a large flat field.

scenes.heading: 103 EXT. MOUNTAINS. DAY. 103
scenes.summary: Daniel and Henry ride through thick forest and plant another wood stake.

scenes.heading: 104 EXT. FERTILE FARM LAND. DAY. 104
scenes.summary: Daniel and Henry plant a wood stake in fertile groves.

scenes.heading: 105 EXT. RIDGE - DAYS LATER. 105
scenes.summary: Daniel and Henry ride over a ridge revealing the Pacific Ocean; Henry plants another stake into the ground near the shore.

scenes.heading: 106 INT. UNION OIL REFINERY. DAY. 106
scenes.summary: Daniel and Henry tour the Union Oil refinery and are greeted warmly by old friend Press St. Clair, who congratulates Daniel on his strike.

scenes.heading: 107 INT. UNION OIL MEETING HALL/NEAR THE OCEAN- DAY.
scenes.summary: Daniel, Henry, and Union Oil executives finish business discussions and Press tells a joke about wives, Standard Oil, and whores.

scenes.heading: 108 INT. PACIFIC OCEAN. DUSK.
scenes.summary: Daniel and Henry swim in the Pacific Ocean at dusk; Daniel describes wanting a beautiful house like one from Fond du Lac when he was a boy, but says seeing it now would make him sick.

scenes.heading: 109 EXT. SEA SHORE. DUSK. 109
scenes.summary: Daniel and Henry emerge naked from the ocean at the shoreline and move toward their clothes and horses.

scenes.heading: 110 INT. BAR. NIGHT. LATER.
scenes.summary: Daniel watches Henry from across a bar as Henry drunkenly talks with a prostitute.

scenes.heading: 111 EXT. HILLS - DAWN.
scenes.summary: Hungover Henry and Daniel ride back from the ocean retracing their trail; Daniel asks about the woman and suggests Henry inherited their father's promiscuity, then learns Henry has two children in Louisiana.

scenes.heading: 112 EXT. FORREST - DUSK. 112 *
scenes.summary: Henry asks Daniel about selling stock in his company and offers to help as a salesman and broker.

scenes.heading: 114 EXT. SAME - NIGHT.
scenes.summary: Daniel wakes Henry at gunpoint and interrogates him about details of their shared past, which Henry cannot answer, revealing Henry is an impostor who met Daniel's real brother and assumed his identity.
scenes.turningPointNote: Daniel discovers Henry is not his brother but an impostor, shattering the only familial connection he believed he had.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME. DAWN. 120A
scenes.summary: Daniel wakes to find Bandy sitting over him, and Daniel immediately asks to lease Bandy's land and build a pipeline through his property, to which Bandy responds that God has told him what Daniel must do.

scenes.heading: 121 INT. CHURCH OF THE THIRD REVELATION. DAY LATER. 121
scenes.summary: Daniel enters Eli's church and tells Eli he's looking for a fresh start and needs his blessing.

scenes.heading: 122 INT. CHURCH OF THE THIRD REVELATION. DAY. 122
scenes.summary: Eli conducts a packed church service where Daniel publicly confesses his sins on his knees, admits he abandoned his son, and is baptized by Eli who repeatedly strikes him and forces him to declare himself a sinner and that he has abandoned his child.
scenes.turningPointNote: Daniel submits to public humiliation and baptism by Eli to secure the land deal, marking a devastating reversal of power between them.

scenes.heading: 123 EXT. RESERVOIR FULL OF OIL - DAWN.
scenes.summary: A boy rows on a lake of oil at dawn; Fletcher meets with George Reynolds, a teacher at a school for the deaf, offering him two thousand dollars a year to work for Daniel; Fletcher, George, and H.W. travel by train.

scenes.heading: 124 EXT. FIELDS NEAR THE SUNDAY RANCH. DAY.
scenes.summary: Daniel supervises pipeline construction, then rushes to greet H.W. when Fletcher arrives with him and George Reynolds, picking up and embracing his son.

scenes.heading: 125 INT. FANCY RESTAURANT/ISABELLA COUNTY -NIGHT- LATER 125
scenes.summary: Daniel and H.W. sit at a fancy restaurant where Daniel tells his deaf son about business deals and expresses frustration at their inability to communicate, saying he needs H.W.'s help to build the company.

scenes.heading: 126 INT. BASECAMP OIL FIBLDS. DAY. 126*
scenes.summary: H.W. teaches George the sign language of the oil business while Mary learns alongside them at the basecamp.

scenes.heading: 127 INT. TRAIN DEPOT. DAY. WEEKS LATER.
scenes.summary: Eli, dressed for travel, tells Daniel at the train depot that he's going on a mission to spread God's word, and Daniel coldly dismisses his farewell.

scenes.heading: 127A EXT. VARIOUS LOCATIONS. TBD. 127A*
scenes.summary: Various shots of H.W. and H.W. with Mary, ending with H.W. running near magic hour.

scenes.heading: 128A EXT./INT. P~~BW MANSION - DUSK.
scenes.summary: Static images show Daniel Plainview's gothic mansion estate with its rooms, bowling alley, and unpacked boxes.

scenes.heading: 128B INT. KITCBBN. PLAINVIEW MANSION. DAY. 128B*
scenes.summary: Daniel signs checks in the kitchen while Al Rose assists him and a servant works in the background.

scenes.heading: 128C INT. HALLWAY. NIGHT. 128C*
scenes.summary: Daniel walks down a hallway at night.

scenes.heading: 128D INT. BEDROOM. MORNING. 128D*
scenes.summary: Daniel sleeps while H.W.'s voiceover speaks of knowing the love of his life since childhood; H.W. and Mary, now aged 25, exchange wedding vows at a church altar while H.W. reflects on idolizing Mary and the darkening of the other side of his life.
scenes.turningPointNote: H.W. marries Mary, establishing his own life separate from Daniel and foreshadowing the coming rupture.

scenes.heading: 129 INT. PRIVATE RAIL CAR. DAY. MOVING.
scenes.summary: H.W. dictates a letter through George to a secretary on a moving train, criticizing his father's business practices and warning of a downfall ahead, while asking for a meeting.

scenes.heading: 129A EXT. NEW SUNDAY RANCH BOOSE. AFTERNOON.
scenes.summary: H.W. and Mary visit the Sunday family at their ranch surrounded by derricks, where Fletcher arrives and has an intense private conversation with H.W. and George while Mary watches from a distance.

scenes.heading: 129B EXT. SUNDAY BOUSE. DUSK.
scenes.summary: Fletcher, H.W., and George have an intense conversation away from the others, observed from a distance.

scenes.heading: 129C INT. ROOM. NIGHT. (TBD) (maybe?)
scenes.summary: Mary and H.W. kiss each other at night.

scenes.heading: 135 INT. PLAINVIEW MANSION - FOYER/HALLWAY - DAY (LATER) 13~
scenes.summary: H.W. and George walk down the long dark hallway of the Plainview mansion and arrive at Daniel's office.

scenes.heading: 136 INT. OFFICE. MOMENTS LATER.
scenes.summary: H.W. tells Daniel he loves him but is leaving to go to Mexico with Mary to start his own oil company with Fletcher Hamilton, and Daniel responds by disowning him, declaring H.W. is an orphan he found in a basket and that he has no son.
scenes.turningPointNote: Daniel disowns H.W., severing the central relationship of the film and completing Daniel's descent into total isolation.

scenes.heading: 137 INT. STAIRWBLL. LATER. 137
scenes.summary: Daniel staggers through a dark doorway and descends deep inside his house.

scenes.heading: 138 INT. BOWLING ALLBY/PLAIHVID MANSIOB. HIGHT 138
scenes.summary: Eli finds Daniel passed out drunk in his bowling alley and wakes him, then reveals he needs money because his congregation has abandoned him and asks Daniel to help him drill on the Bandy tract, but Daniel mocks him, forces Eli to renounce God and admit he's a false prophet, then beats Eli to death with a bowling pin.
scenes.turningPointNote: Daniel murders Eli in a final eruption of rage and nihilism, completing his total moral collapse.
